concatenation","user":{"login":"rishic3","id":77904151,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc3OTA0MTUx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/77904151?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3","html_url":"https://github.com/rishic3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":472944224,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw0NzI5NDQyMjQ=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/labels/cat:bug","name":"cat:bug","color":"ee0701","default":false,"description":"Bugs"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-08T21:45:00Z","updated_at":"2024-11-08T21:45:00Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Description

For sparse matrices, if the row index exceeds int32 max val (2147483647), the index dtype should be promoted to int64 so that the index can hold the data.
However, when concatenating sparse CSR matrices such that the resultant row index exceeds int32 max val, the dtype does not get promoted. This behavior is unique to cupyx and does not occur with scipy. 

### To Reproduce

The scipy version works as expected:

```py
import numpy as np
import scipy
import math

int32_maxval = int('0x7FFFFFFF', 16)
n_rows = math.ceil(int32_maxval / 3)

a = scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=np.float32, format="csr")
b = scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=np.float32, format="csr")
c = scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=np.float32, format="csr")

assert a.indices.dtype == b.indices.dtype == c.indices.dtype == np.int32

concated = scipy.sparse.vstack([a, b, c], format="csr")

assert concated.indices.dtype == np.int64
```
...but cupyx fails:
```py
import cupy as cp
import cupyx
import math

int32_maxval = int('0x7FFFFFFF', 16)
n_rows = math.ceil(int32_maxval / 3)

a = cupyx.scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=cp.float32, format="csr")
b = cupyx.scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=cp.float32, format="csr")
c = cupyx.scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=cp.float32, format="csr")

assert a.indices.dtype == b.indices.dtype == c.indices.dtype == cp.int32

concated = cupyx.scipy.sparse.vstack([a, b, c], format="csr")

assert concated.indices.dtype == cp.int64, "The index dtype should have been promoted to int64"
```

### Installation

Source (`pip install cupy`)

```
OS                           : Linux-6.8.0-45-generic-x86_64-with-glibc2.39
Python Version               : 3.10.15
```

{"action":"closed","number":8732,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/pulls/8732","id":2170541591,"node_id":"PR_kwDOBFKgkM6BX9YX","html_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/pull/8732","diff_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/pull/8732.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/pull/8732.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8732","number":8732,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"Avoid hardcoding index dtypes as int32 for CS matrices","user":{"login":"rishic3","id":77904151,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc3OTA0MTUx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/77904151?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3","html_url":"https://github.com/rishic3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rishic3/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Hi community! This is my first time getting involved with cupy. 
My team encountered issue #8731, where sparse matrices whose indices exceed the int32 maxval should have their indices cast to int64 to avoid overflow.

These changes add an additional check to the CS matrix constructor to check if the index dtype should be promoted, as opposed to hardcoding as int32 (shortcut 'i'). Fixes #8731. 

Happy to add tests for these overflow cases but they would require significant CUDA mem. Description

For sparse matrices, if the row index exceeds int32 max val (2147483647), the index dtype should be promoted to int64 so that the index can hold the data.
However, when concatenating sparse CSR matrices such that the resultant row index exceeds int32 max val, the dtype does not get promoted. This behavior is unique to cupyx and does not occur with scipy. 

### To Reproduce

The scipy version works as expected:

```py
import numpy as np
import scipy
import math

int32_maxval = int('0x7FFFFFFF', 16)
n_rows = math.ceil(int32_maxval / 3)

a = scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=np.float32, format="csr")
b = scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=np.float32, format="csr")
c = scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=np.float32, format="csr")

assert a.indices.dtype == b.indices.dtype == c.indices.dtype == np.int32

concated = scipy.sparse.vstack([a, b, c], format="csr")

assert concated.indices.dtype == np.int64
```
...but cupyx fails:
```py
import cupy as cp
import cupyx
import math

int32_maxval = int('0x7FFFFFFF', 16)
n_rows = math.ceil(int32_maxval / 3)

a = cupyx.scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=cp.float32, format="csr")
b = cupyx.scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=cp.float32, format="csr")
c = cupyx.scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=cp.float32, format="csr")

assert a.indices.dtype == b.indices.dtype == c.indices.dtype == cp.int32

concated = cupyx.scipy.sparse.vstack([a, b, c], format="csr")

assert concated.indices.dtype == cp.int64, "The index dtype should have been promoted to int64"
```

### Installation

Source (`pip install cupy`)

Description

For sparse matrices, if the row index exceeds int32 max val (2147483647), the index dtype should be promoted to int64 so that the index can hold the data.
However, when concatenating sparse CSR matrices such that the resultant row index exceeds int32 max val, the dtype does not get promoted. This behavior is unique to cupyx and does not occur with scipy. 

### To Reproduce

The scipy version works as expected:

```py
import numpy as np
import scipy
import math

int32_maxval = int('0x7FFFFFFF', 16)
n_rows = math.ceil(int32_maxval / 3)

a = scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=np.float32, format="csr")
b = scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=np.float32, format="csr")
c = scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=np.float32, format="csr")

assert a.indices.dtype == b.indices.dtype == c.indices.dtype == np.int32

concated = scipy.sparse.vstack([a, b, c], format="csr")

assert concated.indices.dtype == np.int64
```
...but cupyx fails:
```py
import cupy as cp
import cupyx
import math

int32_maxval = int('0x7FFFFFFF', 16)
n_rows = math.ceil(int32_maxval / 3)

a = cupyx.scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=cp.float32, format="csr")
b = cupyx.scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=cp.float32, format="csr")
c = cupyx.scipy.sparse.random(n_rows, 1, density=0.00001, dtype=cp.float32, format="csr")

assert a.indices.dtype == b.indices.dtype == c.indices.dtype == cp.int32

concated = cupyx.scipy.sparse.vstack([a, b, c], format="csr")

assert concated.indices.dtype == cp.int64, "The index dtype should have been promoted to int64"
```

### Installation

Source (`pip install cupy`)
